creator: archie@whistle.com
text:
Nate Williams writes:
> > >Number:         10165
> > >Category:       bin
> > >Synopsis:       CVS does not know to ignore .So files
> ...
> > 	CVS update knows to ignore certain files. By ignore, that
> > 	means don't show them with a question mark. Since we changed
> > 	our shared object extension from .so to .So, cvs needs to
> > 	be updated to know to ignore files with .So extensions.
> 
> This should be changed in CVSROOT/cvsignore.  It shouldn't require any
> modifications to CVS.  Rather than change it, I'll leave it up to the
> CVS-meisters unless they give me the go ahead.

OK..

But shouldn't the default for FreeBSD be to ignore .So, regardless
of the place where this is actually implemented?

-Archie
